text.title: Frontend Component Practices Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the practices-frontend-components methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each component MUST own exactly one responsibility. Components >150 lines or >5 distinct concerns MUST be split.
rule.rationale: Fat components are unreusable, untestable, and hard to read. Single-responsibility units compose into the page.

rule.statement: Props MUST be typed via TS interface (preferred) or PropTypes. {...rest} prop-spreading is forbidden across DOM boundaries.
rule.rationale: Untyped props let invalid shapes through silently. Prop-spreading to DOM elements leaks framework-specific keys.

rule.statement: Interactive elements MUST have a semantic role + accessible name. role, aria-label/aria-labelledby, keyboard handlers (Enter/Space) are required from the first commit, not bolted on later.
rule.rationale: Bolted-on a11y is incomplete; testing-library + axe-core can verify from the first test if it's there from the start.

rule.statement: A stateful component MUST be either controlled (parent owns value + onChange) or uncontrolled (defaultValue + ref). Both modes simultaneously are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed modes cause double-source-of-truth bugs where the displayed value drifts from the prop.

rule.statement: Component styles MUST be scoped (CSS Modules / scoped <style> / styled-components / Tailwind utility composition). Global selectors that target tag names are forbidden inside component files.

rationale.source: CSS Modules docs + Vue scoped style docs

rule.rationale: Global styles leak across components and break the encapsulation that makes the component portable.

rule.statement: Each component MUST ship with a sibling <Component>.test.tsx (Testing Library) and a sibling <Component>.stories.tsx (Storybook or equivalent).
rule.rationale: Tests + stories are the contract; without them the component drifts and visual regressions go unnoticed.
